matchers.value: ^The Microsoft Exchange IMAP4 service is ready\.?$
^The? Microsoft Exchange POP3 service is ready\.?$
^([^ ]{1,512}) +ESMTP Server \(Microsoft Exchange Internet Mail Service (\d+\.\d+\.\d+\.\d+)\) ready *$
matchers.version_extract: ^([^ ]{1,512}) +ESMTP Server \(Microsoft Exchange Internet Mail Service (\d+\.\d+\.\d+\.\d+)\) ready *$
matchers.note: Microsoft Exchange Server 5.5 and above (for sure, can't be confused with the IIS builtin SMTP service)

matchers.value: ^([^ ]{1,512}) Microsoft Exchange Internet Mail Service (\d+\.\d+\.\d+\.\d+) ready *$
matchers.version_extract: ^([^ ]{1,512}) Microsoft Exchange Internet Mail Service (\d+\.\d+\.\d+\.\d+) ready *$
matchers.note: Microsoft Exchange Server 5.0 (for sure, can't be confused with the IIS builtin SMTP service)

matchers.value: ^([^ ]{1,512}) Microsoft ESMTP MAIL Service ready at
matchers.note: Microsoft Exchange 2007/2010 (for sure, can't be confused with the IIS builtin SMTP service)

examples: The Microsoft Exchange IMAP4 service is ready.
NNTP Service Microsoft. Internet Services 5.00 Version: 5.0.2068.0 Posting Allowed
NNTP Service Microsoft. Internet Services 5.00.7515. Version: 5.0.0.7515 Posting Allowed
NNTP Service Microsoft. Internet Services 5.5 Version: 5.5.1726.0 Posting Allowed
Microsoft Exchange POP3 server version 5.5.2654.50 ready
The Microsoft Exchange POP3 service is ready.
foo.bar ESMTP Server (Microsoft Exchange Internet Mail Service 5.5.2653.13) ready
foo.bar Microsoft Exchange Internet Mail Service 5.0.1460.8 ready
foo.bar Microsoft ESMTP MAIL Service ready at Wed, 21 Jul 2010 19:04:24 -0700
